Title: Verify successful user login within 2 seconds under normal network conditions.
Preconditions: A valid user account (e.g., 'testuser/password123') exists and is active. | The application server is running and accessible. | Network conditions are normal (e.g., typical latency < 100ms, bandwidth > 10Mbps, packet loss < 0.1%).
Steps: Navigate to the application's login page. | Enter the valid username 'testuser' into the username field. | Enter the valid password 'password123' into the password field. | Click the 'Login' button. | Start a timer immediately after clicking 'Login'. | Stop the timer when the user is successfully redirected to the dashboard/home page and the primary content of the page is fully rendered.
ExpectedResult: The user is successfully logged in and redirected to the dashboard/home page. The total time measured from clicking 'Login' to full page render/redirection is less than or equal to 2 seconds. No error messages are displayed.

Title: Verify failed login attempt with incorrect credentials displays error quickly.
Preconditions: A valid user account exists (e.g., 'testuser/password123'). | The application server is running and accessible. | Network conditions are normal (e.g., typical latency < 100ms, bandwidth > 10Mbps, packet loss < 0.1%).
Steps: Navigate to the application's login page. | Enter a valid username 'testuser' into the username field. | Enter an incorrect password 'wrongpassword' into the password field. | Click the 'Login' button. | Start a timer immediately after clicking 'Login'. | Stop the timer when an appropriate error message is displayed on the login page.
ExpectedResult: An appropriate error message (e.g., 'Invalid username or password') is displayed on the login page. The user is not logged in or redirected. The total time measured from clicking 'Login' to error message display is less than or equal to 2 seconds.

Title: Verify failed login attempt with a locked/disabled account displays error quickly.
Preconditions: A user account (e.g., 'lockeduser/password123') exists and is intentionally locked or disabled (e.g., due to multiple failed attempts or administrative action). | The application server is running and accessible. | Network conditions are normal (e.g., typical latency < 100ms, bandwidth > 10Mbps, packet loss < 0.1%).
Steps: Navigate to the application's login page. | Enter the username 'lockeduser' into the username field. | Enter the correct password 'password123' into the password field. | Click the 'Login' button. | Start a timer immediately after clicking 'Login'. | Stop the timer when an appropriate error message is displayed on the login page.
ExpectedResult: An appropriate error message (e.g., 'Your account is locked. Please contact support.' or 'Account disabled.') is displayed on the login page. The user is not logged in or redirected. The total time measured from clicking 'Login' to error message display is less than or equal to 2 seconds.

Title: Verify login response time under expected concurrent user load.
Preconditions: Multiple valid and unique user accounts are available for load testing. | The application server is running and accessible. | Network conditions are normal (e.g., typical latency < 100ms, bandwidth > 10Mbps, packet loss < 0.1%). | A load testing tool is configured to simulate 'N' concurrent users (e.g., 100, 500, or peak expected load).
Steps: Configure the load testing tool to simulate 'N' concurrent users attempting to log in simultaneously or within a short ramp-up period (e.g., 10 users/second for 10 seconds). | Each simulated user performs the following steps: |   a. Navigate to the login page. |   b. Enter unique valid credentials. |   c. Click the 'Login' button. |   d. Measure the response time from button click to successful redirection/page render.
ExpectedResult: All simulated users are successfully logged in. The average, 90th percentile, and 95th percentile response times for successful logins are less than or equal to 2 seconds. The system remains stable with no errors or timeouts during the load test.

Title: Verify successful login with Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) within 2 seconds.
Preconditions: A valid user account (e.g., 'mfauser/password123') exists and has MFA enabled. | The MFA mechanism (e.g., OTP generator, email/SMS service) is functional and accessible. | The application server is running and accessible. | Network conditions are normal (e.g., typical latency < 100ms, bandwidth > 10Mbps, packet loss < 0.1%).
Steps: Navigate to the application's login page. | Enter the valid username 'mfauser' into the username field. | Enter the valid password 'password123' into the password field. | Click the 'Login' button. | Start a timer immediately after clicking the initial 'Login' button. | On the MFA challenge screen, obtain and enter the correct MFA code. | Click the 'Verify' or equivalent button for MFA submission. | Stop the timer when the user is successfully redirected to the dashboard/home page and the primary content of the page is fully rendered.
ExpectedResult: The user is successfully logged in and redirected to the dashboard/home page after MFA verification. The total time measured from the initial 'Login' button click to full page render/redirection after MFA is less than or equal to 2 seconds. No error messages are displayed.
